3. Retrieves existing opinions (bank's perspectives)
4. Uses LLM to formulate a contextual answer
5. Returns plain text answer and the facts used

post.description:
Retain memory items with automatic fact extraction.

This is the main endpoint for storing memories. It supports both synchronous and asynchronous processing via the `async` parameter.

**Features:**
- Efficient batch processing
- Automatic fact extraction from natural language
- Entity recognition and linking
- Document tracking with automatic upsert (when document_id is provided)
- Temporal and semantic linking
- Optional asynchronous processing

**The system automatically:**
1. Extracts semantic facts from the content
2. Generates embeddings
3. Deduplicates similar facts
4. Creates temporal, semantic, and entity links
5. Tracks document metadata

**When `async=true`:** Returns immediately after queuing. Use the operations endpoint to monitor progress.

**When `async=false` (default):** Waits for processing to complete.

**Note:** If a memory item has a `document_id` that already exists, the old document and its memory units will be deleted before creating new ones (upsert behavior).

delete.description: Delete memory units for a memory bank. Optionally filter by type (world, experience, opinion) to delete only specific types. This is a destructive operation that cannot be undone. The bank profile (disposition and background) will be preserved.

parameters.name: type

schema.description: Optional fact type filter (world, experience, opinion)
schema.title: Type

parameters.description: Optional fact type filter (world, experience, opinion)

post.description:
Upload files (PDF, DOCX, etc.), convert them to markdown, and retain as memories.

This endpoint handles file upload, conversion, and memory creation in a single operation.

**Features:**
- Supports PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, images (with OCR), audio (with transcription)
- Automatic file-to-markdown conversion using pluggable parsers
- Files stored in object storage (PostgreSQL by default, S3 for production)
- Each file becomes a separate document with optional metadata/tags
- Always processes asynchronously — returns operation IDs immediately

**The system automatically:**
1. Stores uploaded files in object storage
2. Converts files to markdown
3. Creates document records with file metadata
4. Extracts facts and creates memory units (same as regular retain)

Use the operations endpoint to monitor progress.

**Request format:** multipart/form-data with:
- `files`: One or more files to upload
- `request`: JSON string with FileRetainRequest model

**Parser selection:**
- Set `parser` in the request body to override the server default for all files.
- Set `parser` inside a `files_metadata` entry for per-file control.
- Pass a list (e.g. `['iris', 'markitdown']`) to define an ordered fallback chain — each parser is tried in sequence until one succeeds.
- Falls back to the server default (`ATULYA_API_FILE_PARSER`) if not specified.
- Only parsers enabled on the server may be requested; others return HTTP 400.

org.title: Org
org.description: Organization slug; required only when email belongs to many orgs

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: When the content occurred. Accepts an ISO 8601 datetime string (e.g. '2024-01-15T10:30:00Z'), null/omitted (defaults to now), or the special string 'unset' to explicitly store without any timestamp (use this for timeless content such as fictional documents or static reference material).

document_id.title: Document Id
document_id.description: Optional document ID for this memory item.

entities.title: Entities
entities.description: Optional entities to combine with auto-extracted entities.

tags.title: Tags
tags.description: Optional tags for visibility scoping. Memories with tags can be filtered during recall.

observation_scopes.title: ObservationScopes
observation_scopes.description: How to scope observations during consolidation. 'per_tag' runs one consolidation pass per individual tag, creating separate observations for each tag. 'combined' (default) runs a single pass with all tags together. A list of tag lists runs one pass per inner list, giving full control over which combinations to use.

update_mode.title: Update Mode
update_mode.description: How to handle an existing document with the same document_id. 'replace' (default) deletes prior data for the document and reprocesses from scratch. 'append' concatenates the new content onto the existing document text and reprocesses; delta-aware retain skips chunks that did not change so only the new content triggers extraction. Requires document_id.

example.content: Alice mentioned she's working on a new ML model

bank_id.title: Bank Id
bank_id.description: Root bank ID that will become the repo working tree

repo_name.title: Repo Name
repo_name.description: Optional display name for the repo

source_bank_id.title: Source Bank Id
source_bank_id.description: Optional source bank to clone into the root bank

repo_name.title: Repo Name
repo_name.description: Optional display name for the repo

target_bank_id.title: Target Bank Id
target_bank_id.description: Brand-new bank ID that will receive the forked snapshot

target_bank_name.title: Target Bank Name
target_bank_name.description: Optional display name for the new bank

source_branch.title: Source Branch
source_branch.description: Optional source branch to fork from. Defaults to the repo's active branch.

source_commit_id.title: Source Commit Id
source_commit_id.description: Optional source commit to fork from. Mutually exclusive with source_branch.

include_workspace.title: Include Workspace
include_workspace.description: When true, fork the live branch workspace instead of the branch HEAD commit.

enable_repo.title: Enable Repo
enable_repo.description: Enable repo mode on the forked bank after materializing the snapshot.

repo_name.title: Repo Name
repo_name.description: Optional repo display name for the forked bank when enable_repo=true.

commit_id.title: Commit Id
commit_id.description: Commit to reset the active branch to

force.title: Force
force.description: Allow reset even when the workspace is dirty

MentalModelListResponse.title: MentalModelListResponse
MentalModelListResponse.description: Response model for listing mental models.

content.title: Content
content.description: The mental model content as well-formatted markdown (auto-generated from reflect endpoint)

reflect_response.title: Reflect Response
reflect_response.description: Full reflect API response payload including based_on facts and observations

MentalModelResponse.title: MentalModelResponse
MentalModelResponse.description: Response model for a mental model (stored reflect response).

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: Refresh mode. 'full' (default) regenerates the entire mental model from the reflect synthesis on every refresh. 'delta' performs surgical edits against the stored structured representation, leaving sections that no new fact contradicts physically untouched (no LLM-mediated re-emission of unchanged content). If the mental model has no existing content, or if the source_query has changed since the last refresh, delta mode falls back to a full regeneration automatically.

refresh_after_consolidation.title: Refresh After Consolidation
refresh_after_consolidation.description: If true, refresh this mental model after observations consolidation (real-time mode)

MentalModelTrigger.title: MentalModelTrigger
MentalModelTrigger.description: Trigger settings for a mental model.

MinScores.title: MinScores
MinScores.description: Minimum scores for filtering recall results (all inclusive, AND-ed).

OperationProgressResponse.title: OperationProgressResponse
OperationProgressResponse.description: Typed progress payload for async operations when totals are known.

OperationResponse.title: OperationResponse
OperationResponse.description: Response model for a single async operation.

OperationResultResponse.title: OperationResultResponse
OperationResultResponse.description: Response model for retrieving the final result of an async operation.

stage.title: Stage
stage.description: High-level progress stage for pending async work, derived from result_metadata.operation_stage.

progress.description: Typed progress counters when the operation exposes known totals.

result_metadata.title: Result Metadata
result_metadata.description: Internal metadata for debugging. Structure may change without notice. Not for production use.

child_operations.title: Child Operations
child_operations.description: Child operations for batch operations (if applicable)

OperationStatusResponse.title: OperationStatusResponse
OperationStatusResponse.description: Response model for getting a single operation status.

OperationsListResponse.title: OperationsListResponse
OperationsListResponse.description: Response model for list operations endpoint.

branch_name.title: Branch Name
branch_name.description: Optional memory repo branch to query without checking it out.

types.title: Types
types.description: List of fact types to recall: 'world', 'experience', 'observation'. Defaults to world and experience if not specified.

include.description: Options for including additional data (entities are included by default)

tags.title: Tags
tags.description: Filter memories by tags. If not specified, all memories are returned.

tags_match.title: Tags Match
tags_match.description: How to match tags: 'any' (OR, includes untagged), 'all' (AND, includes untagged), 'any_strict' (OR, excludes untagged), 'all_strict' (AND, excludes untagged).

tag_groups.title: Tag Groups
tag_groups.description: Compound boolean tag predicates. Each entry is a leaf (``{"tags": [...], "match": "any|all|any_strict|all_strict"}``) or a nested group (``{"and": [...]}``, ``{"or": [...]}``, ``{"not": {...}}``). Top-level entries are AND-ed together. Mutually exclusive with `tags`.

prefer_observations.title: Prefer Observations
prefer_observations.description: Prefer observation results during recall ranking.

example.query: What did Alice say about machine learning?

entities.title: Entities
entities.description: Entity states for entities mentioned in results

chunks.title: Chunks
chunks.description: Chunks for facts, keyed by chunk_id

source_facts.title: Source Facts
source_facts.description: Source facts for observation-type results, keyed by fact ID

456e7890-e12b-34d5-a678-901234567890.text: Alice works at Google on the AI team. She's been there for 3 years...

observations.text: Alice works at Google on the AI team

results.text: Alice works at Google on the AI team

trace.query: What did Alice say about machine learning?

example.text: Alice works at Google on the AI team

memories.title: Memories
memories.description: Memory facts used to generate the response

ReflectBasedOn.title: ReflectBasedOn
ReflectBasedOn.description: Evidence the response is based on: memories, mental models, and directives.

text.title: Text
text.description: Fact text. When type='observation', this contains markdown-formatted consolidated knowledge

ReflectFact.title: ReflectFact
ReflectFact.description: A fact used in think response.

facts.description: Include facts that the answer is based on. Set to {} to enable, null to disable (default: disabled).

tool_calls.description: Include tool calls trace. Set to {} for full trace (input+output), {output: false} for inputs only.

ReflectIncludeOptions.title: ReflectIncludeOptions
ReflectIncludeOptions.description: Options for including additional data in reflect results.

scope.title: Scope
scope.description: Call scope: agent_1, agent_2, final, etc.

ReflectLLMCall.title: ReflectLLMCall
ReflectLLMCall.description: An LLM call made during reflect agent execution.

ReflectMentalModel.title: ReflectMentalModel
ReflectMentalModel.description: A mental model used during reflect.

branch_name.title: Branch Name
branch_name.description: Optional memory repo branch to query without checking it out.

context.title: Context
context.description: DEPRECATED: Additional context is now concatenated with the query. Pass context directly in the query field instead. If provided, it will be appended to the query for backward compatibility.

include.description: Options for including additional data (disabled by default)

response_schema.title: Response Schema
response_schema.description: Optional JSON Schema for structured output. When provided, the response will include a 'structured_output' field with the LLM response parsed according to this schema.

tags.title: Tags
tags.description: Filter memories by tags during reflection. If not specified, all memories are considered.

tags_match.title: Tags Match
tags_match.description: How to match tags: 'any' (OR, includes untagged), 'all' (AND, includes untagged), 'any_strict' (OR, excludes untagged), 'all_strict' (AND, excludes untagged).

tag_groups.title: Tag Groups
tag_groups.description: Compound boolean tag predicates. Each entry is a leaf (``{"tags": [...], "match": "any|all|any_strict|all_strict"}``) or a nested group (``{"and": [...]}``, ``{"or": [...]}``, ``{"not": {...}}``). Top-level entries are AND-ed together. Mutually exclusive with `tags`.

example.query: What do you think about artificial intelligence?

text.title: Text
text.description: The reflect response as well-formatted markdown (headers, lists, bold/italic, code blocks, etc.)

based_on.description: Evidence used to generate the response. Only present when include.facts is set.

structured_output.title: Structured Output
structured_output.description: Structured output parsed according to the request's response_schema. Only present when response_schema was provided in the request.

usage.description: Token usage metrics for LLM calls during reflection.

trace.description: Execution trace of tool and LLM calls. Only present when include.tool_calls is set.

memories.text: I discussed AI applications last week

example.text:
## AI Overview

Based on my understanding, AI is a **transformative technology**:

- Used extensively in healthcare
- Discussed in recent conversations
- Continues to evolve rapidly

tool.title: Tool
tool.description: Tool name: lookup, recall, learn, expand

output.title: Output
output.description: Tool output (only included when include.tool_calls.output is true)

iteration.title: Iteration
iteration.description: Iteration number (1-based) when this tool was called

ReflectToolCall.title: ReflectToolCall
ReflectToolCall.description: A tool call made during reflect agent execution.

ReflectTrace.title: ReflectTrace
ReflectTrace.description: Execution trace of LLM and tool calls during reflection.

async.title: Async
async.description: If true, process asynchronously in background. If false, wait for completion (default: false)

async.title: Async
async.description: Whether the operation was processed asynchronously

operation_id.title: Operation Id
operation_id.description: Operation ID for tracking async operations. Use GET /v1/default/banks/{bank_id}/operations to list operations. Only present when async=true.

usage.description: Token usage metrics for LLM calls during fact extraction (only present for synchronous operations)

RetryOperationResponse.title: RetryOperationResponse
RetryOperationResponse.description: Response model for retry operation endpoint.

max_tokens.title: Max Tokens
max_tokens.description: Maximum total tokens for source facts across all observations (-1 = unlimited)

max_tokens_per_observation.title: Max Tokens Per Observation
max_tokens_per_observation.description: Maximum tokens of source facts per observation (-1 = unlimited)

SourceFactsIncludeOptions.title: SourceFactsIncludeOptions
SourceFactsIncludeOptions.description: Options for including source facts for observation-type results.

TagGroupAnd.title: TagGroupAnd
TagGroupAnd.description: Logical AND over a list of nested groups.

TagGroupLeaf.title: TagGroupLeaf
TagGroupLeaf.description:
A single tag predicate, semantically identical to the flat `tags` field.

`tags` is a non-empty list of tag strings; `match` controls AND/OR semantics
and whether untagged memories are included, exactly mirroring the existing
`TagsMatch` enum.

TagGroupNot.title: TagGroupNot
TagGroupNot.description: Logical NOT around a single nested group.

TagGroupOr.title: TagGroupOr
TagGroupOr.description: Logical OR over a list of nested groups.

count.title: Count
count.description: Number of memories with this tag

TokenUsage.title: TokenUsage
TokenUsage.description:
Token usage metrics for LLM calls.

Tracks input/output tokens for a single request to enable
per-request cost tracking and monitoring.

output.title: Output
output.description: Include tool outputs in the trace. Set to false to only include inputs (smaller payload).

ToolCallsIncludeOptions.title: ToolCallsIncludeOptions
ToolCallsIncludeOptions.description: Options for including tool calls in reflect results.

TrajectoryViterbiStepResponse.description: One step on the decoded Viterbi path.
